Header.Name: Person
Header.Description: This is a test profile for registering Person-related data according to the PiCo and CIDOC-CRM data models. It is inspired by: https://docs.swissartresearch.net/et/persons/. It is not strictly compliant with those models, but it aims to operationalize both approaches for data entry. From PiCo it implements the concept of "Observation". It follows PiCo's main principle that "A person observation represents how a person is recorded on a Source. Therefore, it must always be related to a source. Person observations closely reflect data as it appears in the source, that is, with the age, occupation, and spelling of the person's name recorded as in the record." However, this Profile can also be used for "Reconstructions" or Person-related data in general: - If the aim is to record observations from ONE source: enter the source's basic metadata, and use the profile as it is, where there are components and elements to register "labels" as observed, plus confidence for those observations. Make sure that the cardinality of the Source component is 1-1 in your local implementation. - If the aim is to record person-related data combined from different sources, without the need to keep provenance for every single element, only for the aggregated data in the record, use "Source" to enter all the sources used to extract the data. Also, the elements/components that keep provenance at each level can be removed in the local implementation. Make sure that the cardinality of the Source component is 1-unbounded in your local implementation.

Component.name: Person
Component.Documentation: A Person is a human being whose individual existence can somehow be documented. Container class for Person observation and Person reconstruction. https://personsincontext.org/model#Person

Element.name: name
Element.Documentation: The name of the item. (Also used for titles).

Element.name: urlZoteroItem
Element.Documentation: Enter the Zotero group link to the record (e.g., https://www.zotero.org/groups/6512312/tastade/items/JHFS57PB)

Element.name: urlPageScan
Element.Documentation: A link to the location of the scan at the Tastade archive. A link to scan as it can be displayed online and a link to a thumbnail (for search results) should be provided. Preferably a link to a IIIF manifest.

Element.name: position
Element.Documentation: (to be defined: use for page number?), PiCo says: "The position defines the order in which scans are presented on the web. This feature is required when a Source has multiple scans."

Element.name: hasRole
Element.Documentation: The role of a Person observation on the Source. Use a term from the PiCo Roles thesaurus: https://terms.personsincontext.org/roles

Component.name: Identifiers
Component.Documentation: ->P1->E42[1_1]->P190->rdf:Literal. Internal note: for Letters (profile v2), the URI should be mapped to: https://lod.academy/cer/vocab/ontology#21_Identifier

Element.name: identifierType
Element.Documentation: The type/scheme of identifier (e.g., "Wikidata QID", "VIAF", "project local"

Element.name: identifierValue
Element.Documentation: Add the identifier's label (e.g., the shelfmark)

Element.name: identifierUri
Element.Documentation: Persistent identifier (optional): If the object has a permanent identifier (e.g., a URN, DOI, or handle), enter the full IRI here (e.g., https://resolver.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/SBB00019E8400000003). It does not need to be resolvable. If it is OpenStreetMaps: Enter the URI from OpenStreetMap API. You can search directly in OpenStreetMaps and paste the URI here. The URI has to have this format, for example: https://www.openstreetmap.org/relation/214885 (note the type in the middle: relation, node, way)

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: fullName
Element.Documentation: For persons, it maps to: https://w3id.org/pnv#literalName

Element.name: nameType
Element.Documentation: The type of name. Use a value from the name type vocabulary per entity type (e.g., for persons: current name, former name, alias, etc.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: PersonNamePart
Component.Documentation: person_7_name_part / Name Part / This field is used to indicate a type of a specific part of the name attributed to the documented person. / ->P1->E33_E41[5_1]->P106->E33_E41[7_1]->rdfs:label->rdf:Literal

Element.name: prefix
Element.Documentation: A Prefix is a noble or honorific title prefixing a name, e.g. 'Prof. dr.' or 'Jhr.'

Element.name: initials
Element.Documentation: An initial is the first letter of a person's given name (or sometimes a combination of letters). The Initials property can be used to separately record the initial(s) of a person's given name(s). Each initial should be followed by a dot. It is recommended to always use the Initials property to record initials, but if only the initials of a given name are known, they may also be recorded using pnv:givenName (or pnv:firstName).

Element.name: givenName
Element.Documentation: A Given name designates the name(s) given to someone at birth or any other name-giving event. In the U.S., the first name of a Person.

Element.name: patronym
Element.Documentation: A Patronym is a name element based on the given name of one's father or grandfather. This is also the preferred property for a matronym (i.e. a name element based on the given name of one's mother or grandmother).

Element.name: givenNameSufix
Element.Documentation: A Given name suffix is a name part that is appended to a given name. In the name of the fifteenth-century Duke of Burgundy Philip the Good, for example, 'the Good' is the Given name suffix.

Element.name: infixTitle
Element.Documentation: An Infix title designates a title in the middle of a name.

Element.name: surname
Element.Documentation: A surname is the name of the family a person is born into, including one or more prefixes. In some cases the honorific prefix can be incorporated into the surname (e.g. Van Welderen baron Rengers).

Element.name: surnamePrefix
Element.Documentation: A Surname prefix is a name part prefixing the surname (e.g. 'van' or 'van den').

Element.name: baseSurname
Element.Documentation: A Base surname is a family name without any prefixes, if those prefixes need to be ignored in sorting. If the family name is e.g. 'de Vries' and it needs to be sorted under 'V', the Base surname is 'Vries'.

Element.name: trailingPatronym
Element.Documentation: A Trailing patronym refers to the parent's given name, suffixing the person's family name, mostly used for disambiguation.

Element.name: honorificSuffix
Element.Documentation: An honorific suffix following a Person's name such as M.D./PhD/MSCSW.

Element.name: disambiguatingDescription
Element.Documentation: A Disambiguating description is a name part that is appended to make sure that the right person is designated. Name parts like 'Sr.' and 'Jr.', used to distinguish a child from his parent, are disambiguating descriptions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: fullName
Element.Documentation: For persons, it maps to: https://w3id.org/pnv#literalName

Element.name: nameType
Element.Documentation: The type of name. Use a value from the name type vocabulary per entity type (e.g., for persons: current name, former name, alias, etc.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range. Follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: :Add any clarifying notes related to the dates. These notes may be for internal or external use (t.b.d.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range. Follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: :Add any clarifying notes related to the dates. These notes may be for internal or external use (t.b.d.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range. Follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: :Add any clarifying notes related to the dates. These notes may be for internal or external use (t.b.d.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: FloruitPeriods
Component.Documentation: This component is used to record the dates of the documented period of activity/flourishing of the documented person. It's also used for life "hints" (years when a person was known to be active). See also: https://docs.swissartresearch.net/et/persons/

Element.name: dateAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Enter the date exactly as it is written in the source (trascription or extraction), but not yet normalized.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range. Follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: :Add any clarifying notes related to the dates. These notes may be for internal or external use (t.b.d.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: category
Element.Documentation: Type of relationship.

Element.name: description
Element.Documentation: Description or notes clarifying the relationship.

Element.name: date
Element.Documentation: Date in which this relationship took place (if applicable).

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: category
Element.Documentation: Type of relationship.

Element.name: description
Element.Documentation: Description or notes clarifying the relationship.

Element.name: date
Element.Documentation: Date in which this relationship took place (if applicable).

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: LifeEventOther
Component.Documentation: Except Birth and Death, which are modelled separatedly.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: Identifiers
Component.Documentation: ->P1->E42[1_1]->P190->rdf:Literal. Internal note: for Letters (profile v2), the URI should be mapped to: https://lod.academy/cer/vocab/ontology#21_Identifier

Element.name: identifierType
Element.Documentation: The type/scheme of identifier (e.g., "Wikidata QID", "VIAF", "project local"

Element.name: identifierValue
Element.Documentation: Add the identifier's label (e.g., the shelfmark)

Element.name: identifierUri
Element.Documentation: Persistent identifier (optional): If the object has a permanent identifier (e.g., a URN, DOI, or handle), enter the full IRI here (e.g., https://resolver.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/SBB00019E8400000003). It does not need to be resolvable. If it is OpenStreetMaps: Enter the URI from OpenStreetMap API. You can search directly in OpenStreetMaps and paste the URI here. The URI has to have this format, for example: https://www.openstreetmap.org/relation/214885 (note the type in the middle: relation, node, way)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: dateStart
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter a specific date (if there is no date range), follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: dateEnd
Element.Documentation: Use this field to enter the end date if there was a range. Follow the instructions here: https://knaw.sharepoint.com/:w:/s/PRJ-Brievenportaal/IQB7jUVOq98BR4Oy786Ch8n1AcilMidno3GLLVdmgUaYF-8?e=FBjVTA

Element.name: noteDate
Element.Documentation: :Add any clarifying notes related to the dates. These notes may be for internal or external use (t.b.d.)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: category
Element.Documentation: Type of relationship.

Element.name: description
Element.Documentation: Description or notes clarifying the relationship.

Element.name: date
Element.Documentation: Date in which this relationship took place (if applicable).

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: Identifiers
Component.Documentation: ->P1->E42[1_1]->P190->rdf:Literal. Internal note: for Letters (profile v2), the URI should be mapped to: https://lod.academy/cer/vocab/ontology#21_Identifier

Element.name: identifierType
Element.Documentation: The type/scheme of identifier (e.g., "Wikidata QID", "VIAF", "project local"

Element.name: identifierValue
Element.Documentation: Add the identifier's label (e.g., the shelfmark)

Element.name: identifierUri
Element.Documentation: Persistent identifier (optional): If the object has a permanent identifier (e.g., a URN, DOI, or handle), enter the full IRI here (e.g., https://resolver.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/SBB00019E8400000003). It does not need to be resolvable. If it is OpenStreetMaps: Enter the URI from OpenStreetMap API. You can search directly in OpenStreetMaps and paste the URI here. The URI has to have this format, for example: https://www.openstreetmap.org/relation/214885 (note the type in the middle: relation, node, way)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: Identifiers
Component.Documentation: ->P1->E42[1_1]->P190->rdf:Literal. Internal note: for Letters (profile v2), the URI should be mapped to: https://lod.academy/cer/vocab/ontology#21_Identifier

Element.name: identifierType
Element.Documentation: The type/scheme of identifier (e.g., "Wikidata QID", "VIAF", "project local"

Element.name: identifierValue
Element.Documentation: Add the identifier's label (e.g., the shelfmark)

Element.name: identifierUri
Element.Documentation: Persistent identifier (optional): If the object has a permanent identifier (e.g., a URN, DOI, or handle), enter the full IRI here (e.g., https://resolver.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/SBB00019E8400000003). It does not need to be resolvable. If it is OpenStreetMaps: Enter the URI from OpenStreetMap API. You can search directly in OpenStreetMaps and paste the URI here. The URI has to have this format, for example: https://www.openstreetmap.org/relation/214885 (note the type in the middle: relation, node, way)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: Identifiers
Component.Documentation: ->P1->E42[1_1]->P190->rdf:Literal. Internal note: for Letters (profile v2), the URI should be mapped to: https://lod.academy/cer/vocab/ontology#21_Identifier

Element.name: identifierType
Element.Documentation: The type/scheme of identifier (e.g., "Wikidata QID", "VIAF", "project local"

Element.name: identifierValue
Element.Documentation: Add the identifier's label (e.g., the shelfmark)

Element.name: identifierUri
Element.Documentation: Persistent identifier (optional): If the object has a permanent identifier (e.g., a URN, DOI, or handle), enter the full IRI here (e.g., https://resolver.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/SBB00019E8400000003). It does not need to be resolvable. If it is OpenStreetMaps: Enter the URI from OpenStreetMap API. You can search directly in OpenStreetMaps and paste the URI here. The URI has to have this format, for example: https://www.openstreetmap.org/relation/214885 (note the type in the middle: relation, node, way)

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: labelAsWritten
Element.Documentation: Write here the name as observed in the source (as written, transcribed, or extracted). If you are entering data about an entity, but there is no written name for it, state "unknown" or "unnamed" following project internal conventions.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: hasAgeNormalized
Element.Documentation: The age of a person. Only used for Person observations, in case the birth date is not known, but the age is. In combination with de date of the Source, a birth date range can be derived, which can be used in the process of creating a Person reconstruction. The age is considered to be in years, unless otherwise mentioned. So an age of "4" is considered to be 4 years, where "4 months" is interpreted as 4 months. Numerical ages ("4") are preferred above ages in text ("four").

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: category
Element.Documentation: Type of relationship.

Element.name: description
Element.Documentation: Description or notes clarifying the relationship.

Element.name: date
Element.Documentation: Date in which this relationship took place (if applicable).

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Component.name: ReconstructionActivity
Component.Documentation: The Activity that generated a PersonReconstruction. A Person Reconstruction can either be created by a human researcher or by a software algorithm. In case of a human-made reconstruction, provide information about the time, place and knowledge- input. In case of a software algorithm, provide information about the name, version and configuration settings of the program.

Element.name: typeActivity
Element.Documentation: Register which type of activity was used to make the reconstruction (not from PiCo, t.b.d.)

Element.name: agent
Element.Documentation: The agent (person or organisation) that is responsible for the creation of a person reconstruction.

Element.name: startedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The start time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: endedAtTime
Element.Documentation: The end time of the activity that generated a Person Reconstruction.

Element.name: confidenceValue
Element.Documentation: How confident you (or an algorithm) are of an observation (what you observed (read) in the source), or of a reconstruction. Reflect the uncertainties and ambiguities of the inferred observation (or reconstruction) by choosing one of the values.

Element.name: noteType
Element.Documentation: Add any specific types of notes to the controlled vocabulary.
